experience.short_description: Built LLM-powered document intelligence tools for enterprise treasury operations.
experience.summary: Designed a PDF processing pipeline and LLM-powered project intelligence dashboard for multi-region treasury operations at EY, reducing manual document review by ~50%.
highlights: Designed an end-to-end PDF processing pipeline for enterprise treasury documents — layout-aware field extraction, schema normalization, and structured handoff to downstream workflow systems — reducing manual document review by ~50%.

Engineered an LLM-powered project intelligence dashboard that parses long enterprise email chains and extracts structured signals: go-live dates, testing status, dependencies, action items, milestones, and payment information — providing consolidated visibility across multi-region treasury operations.

Layered automated country-level and bank-level categorization with project segmentation logic on extraction output, enabling operations teams to surface and filter project status across entities without manual data entry.

experience.short_description: Developed and optimized a YOLO-based fire detection system end-to-end.
experience.summary: Owned the full CV development lifecycle for a YOLOv8 fire detection system at VertexPlus — improving detection reliability by 18% and reducing false positives by 15%.
highlights: Owned the full CV development lifecycle for a YOLO-based fire detection system: dataset sourcing, bounding box annotation, augmentation strategy, training configuration, evaluation loop, and inference pipeline testing.

Diagnosed systematic false positive patterns through class-level analysis, resolving via targeted dataset re-curation and augmentation tuning — improved detection reliability by 18% and reduced false positive rate by 15%.
Evaluated model behaviour under varied lighting and occlusion conditions, establishing a validation protocol that guided iterative training and improved generalization on out-of-distribution samples.

experience.short_description: Built agentic workflows and RAG pipelines using LangGraph and LangChain.
experience.summary: Built stateful agentic workflows with LangGraph and RAG pipelines with LangChain at Dotsquares, improving response consistency by 25%.
highlights: Built stateful agentic workflows using LangGraph with explicit tool-calling architectures, conditional branching, and multi-turn conversation handling with session-scoped memory management.
Implemented RAG pipelines with LangChain integrating vector retrieval into LLM generation to ground outputs in document context and reduce hallucination — improved response consistency by 25%.

Developed reusable pipeline components for document ingestion, chunking strategy, and retrieval configuration, enabling rapid prototyping of new use cases without rebuilding core workflow scaffolding.

projects.name: LLM Consulate
projects.short_description: Multi-model AI consensus engine with parallel inference and dissent preservation.
projects.summary: Multi-model AI consensus engine that consults a council of open-source LLMs, measures agreement, preserves dissenting viewpoints, and synthesizes answers only when meaningful consensus exists.
projects.description: Multi-model AI consensus engine that consults a council of open-source LLMs, measures agreement, preserves dissenting viewpoints, and synthesizes answers only when meaningful consensus exists.
highlights: Consults a council of open-source LLMs in parallel rather than returning a single model response.
Measures inter-model agreement and preserves dissenting viewpoints when consensus is weak.
Synthesizes answers only when meaningful consensus exists.
Streams results in real time via Server-Sent Events.
Production-grade FastAPI backend with async orchestration.

projects.name: YouTubeGPT
projects.tagline: Full-stack GenAI RAG application for YouTube video Q&A
projects.short_description: Ask questions about any YouTube video using RAG + LLMs.
projects.summary: Full-stack GenAI app that ingests YouTube transcripts, generates dense embeddings into a vector index, and serves context-aware answers via a cyberpunk-themed React frontend.
projects.description: A full-stack Gen-AI application that ingests YouTube transcripts, generates dense embeddings into a vector index, and serves context-aware answers through a React frontend via REST API. Features a cyberpunk-inspired UI with a hacker aesthetic, dark terminal theme, and neon accents.
highlights: Improved answer relevance by 30% over naive retrieval baselines.
Decoupled retrieval quality from generation quality for independent optimization.
Collapsible Q&A history panel with smooth Framer Motion animations.
Structured answer display with headlines, details, evidence, and gap analysis.

projects.name: RoadDefect-Dodge
projects.tagline: YOLOv8 multi-class road defect detector trained from scratch
projects.short_description: Detects potholes, cracks, and manholes using YOLOv8 from scratch.
projects.summary: Trained a YOLOv8 multi-class road defect detector from scratch across the full ML lifecycle — dataset curation to inference — for pothole, crack, and manhole detection.
projects.description: Trained a YOLOv8 multi-class road defect detector from scratch with no pretrained weights across the full ML lifecycle — dataset curation, bounding box annotation, augmentation pipeline, training, validation, and inference for pothole, crack, and manhole detection.
highlights: Full ML lifecycle from scratch — no pretrained weights used.
Configurable confidence thresholds and NMS settings in inference pipeline.
Augmentation strategy designed to handle class imbalance and real-world lighting variation.
Trained simultaneously across all three defect categories.

projects.short_description: Desktop app that uses GitHub repos as cloud photo storage.
projects.summary: A layered desktop application that repurposes GitHub repositories as a photo storage backend with OAuth, thumbnail generation, folder organization, and multithreaded sync.
projects.description: A layered desktop application that repurposes GitHub repositories as a photo storage backend — OAuth authentication, repository lifecycle management, image upload, thumbnail generation, folder organization, and synchronization.
highlights: I/O-bound Git and sync operations offloaded to background worker threads for a non-blocking UI.
Local cache and repository index mapping folder hierarchies to Git tree structures.
Full GitHub OAuth authentication flow.
Thumbnail generation and folder organization built-in.

HuggingFace embedding models run entirely locally.

writings.title: The Encoder-Decoder Architecture: Building Sequence-to-Sequence Models
writings.short_description: A complete walkthrough of the original Seq2Seq encoder-decoder architecture, from context vectors and teacher forcing to training and inference.
writings.summary: A deep dive into the original sequence-to-sequence encoder-decoder architecture, covering tokenization, context vectors, teacher forcing, loss calculation, backpropagation through time, inference, and the key improvements introduced in the original paper.
writings.description: Learn how Seq2Seq encoder-decoder models work under the hood, from LSTM-based encoding and decoding to teacher forcing, cross-entropy loss, BPTT, embeddings, stacked LSTMs, and input sequence reversal.

key_concepts.encoder: Processes the source sequence token by token and compresses the information into a context vector represented by the final hidden state (and cell state for LSTMs).
key_concepts.context_vector: A fixed-size representation of the input sequence that is passed from the encoder to the decoder. It becomes a bottleneck for long sequences.
key_concepts.decoder: Generates the target sequence one token at a time using the context vector and previously generated outputs.
key_concepts.teacher_forcing: During training, the decoder receives the ground-truth token from the dataset as the next input rather than its own prediction, improving convergence and stability.
key_concepts.cross_entropy: Loss is computed at every decoder timestep by comparing predicted probabilities against the correct target token using categorical cross entropy.
key_concepts.bptt: Gradients flow backward through all decoder and encoder timesteps via Backpropagation Through Time, allowing the entire model to learn jointly.
key_concepts.inference: During prediction, the decoder feeds its own previous output back as input until it generates the end token.
key_concepts.limitations: Compressing an entire sequence into a single fixed-size context vector creates an information bottleneck, especially for long sentences.

architecture_improvements.embeddings: Replace sparse one-hot vectors with dense learned representations that are more compact and capture semantic relationships between words.
architecture_improvements.stacked_lstms: Use multiple LSTM layers instead of a single layer to learn increasingly abstract sequence representations.
architecture_improvements.input_reversal: Reverse the source sequence before encoding to shorten dependency paths between corresponding input and output tokens, improving optimization.

writings.title: CNN Transfer Learning: Feature Extraction and Fine-Tuning Explained
writings.short_description: How pre-trained CNNs let you skip training from scratch.
writings.summary: A deep dive into transfer learning — how pre-trained CNNs, feature extraction, and fine-tuning let you adapt deep models to new tasks without training from scratch.
writings.description: How pre-trained CNNs, feature extraction, and fine-tuning let you adapt deep models without training from scratch.

key_concepts.feature_extraction: Freeze the entire convolutional base, train only the new classifier head. Computationally cheap, works well when new task is similar to original.
key_concepts.fine_tuning: Freeze early layers, allow later layers to update. More expensive but better results when tasks differ significantly. Always use a smaller learning rate.
key_concepts.workflow: Step 1: freeze base, train head. Step 2: evaluate. Step 3: unfreeze top conv layers. Step 4: fine-tune at low learning rate.
key_concepts.why_not_scratch: Training from scratch needs millions of labeled images and days of GPU time. Transfer learning starts from a model that already understands the visual world.

writings.title: RAG Explained for Beginners
writings.short_description: Beginner-friendly walkthrough of Retrieval Augmented Generation.
writings.summary: A beginner-friendly walkthrough of RAG — from why LLMs hallucinate to embeddings, chunking, vector databases, similarity search, and the full end-to-end pipeline.
writings.description: A beginner-friendly walkthrough of Retrieval Augmented Generation, from embeddings to the full pipeline.

key_concepts.rag_definition: Retrieve relevant context from a knowledge base, then generate a response grounded in that context. Solves the problem of generic LLMs lacking domain-specific knowledge.
key_concepts.chunk_overlap: Overlap between adjacent chunks preserves context at boundaries — prevents meaning from being cut off mid-idea.
key_concepts.embeddings: Numerical vector representations of text. Similar meanings produce similar vectors. Enables mathematical comparison of semantic content.
key_concepts.similarity_search: Query is converted to an embedding, then compared against all stored chunk embeddings. Most similar chunks become the context fed to the LLM.
key_concepts.top_k: Controls how many retrieved chunks become context. More chunks = more context but more tokens.
key_concepts.persistence: Embeddings calculated once and stored — not recomputed on every query.
misconceptions: RAG does not eliminate hallucinations entirely — it significantly reduces them.
The entire document is not the context — only the top-K retrieved chunks are.
Embeddings do not need to be recalculated every time — persistence handles that.

pipeline_steps: Run similarity search, retrieve top-K chunks
Build final prompt: system prompt + context + question

writings.title: How Bitcoin Is Mined
writings.short_description: Beginner-friendly breakdown of Bitcoin mining and Proof-of-Work.
writings.summary: A beginner-friendly explanation of Bitcoin mining — from transactions and mempools to Proof-of-Work, SHA-256, difficulty adjustment, and the 21M BTC supply cap.
writings.description: A beginner-friendly explanation of Bitcoin mining — from transactions and mempools to Proof-of-Work, SHA-256, and block rewards.

key_concepts.utxo: Unspent Transaction Output — the amount of bitcoin not yet spent from previous transactions.
key_concepts.mempool: Waiting area for pending transactions. Every node maintains its own mempool. Miners pick transactions with the highest fees first.
key_concepts.block_reward: Currently 3.125 BTC, halves every four years. After 2140, miners will earn only transaction fees.
key_concepts.target: 256-bit number defining maximum allowable hash value. Auto-adjusts every 2016 blocks (~2 weeks) to maintain one block per 10 minutes.
key_concepts.nonce: 32-bit number in the block header that miners vary to find a hash below the target.
key_concepts.sha256: Bitcoin uses double SHA-256 on the block header. Each attempt generates a 256-bit hash. Valid when hash ≤ target.
key_concepts.supply_cap: 21 million BTC total. Controlled by the halving schedule to prevent inflation.
